criterion: Resets each chaser enemy to its initial position and idle state after player respawn.

forms.assessment_process: Trigger a death, then observe each chaser returns to its start transform and stops chasing. Compare against their initial placement in platformer.tscn.
forms.explanation: Enemy state carryover can cause unfair immediate re-deaths and breaks expected checkpoint behavior in platformers.

criterion: Respawns the player without reloading the current scene and within 1 frame of the death trigger.

forms.assessment_process: Trigger death and confirm the current scene node path stays the same and no reload occurs. Verify the player position updates by the next physics frame.
forms.explanation: Avoiding scene reload preserves runtime state and prevents hitches, which is central to the requested experience.

criterion: Triggers fall death only when the player drops below the lowest walkable surface by a safe margin.

forms.assessment_process: Walk and jump across the level and confirm no false deaths occur. Fall off the lowest platform and confirm death triggers shortly after leaving the play area.
forms.explanation: A fall detector must avoid false positives while still preventing long out-of-bounds time that feels broken.

criterion: Uses a decoupled mechanism, signals or groups, so enemies and projectiles do not store a persistent player reference.

forms.assessment_process: Inspect enemy and projectile scripts and confirm they emit a signal or use groups instead of caching the player node. Verify death still triggers on contact and hit.
forms.explanation: Loose coupling reduces brittle dependencies and makes it easier to reuse enemies and projectiles across levels.

criterion: Resets player velocity to zero and restores immediate movement control on the first frame after respawn.

forms.assessment_process: Die while moving, then confirm velocity reads as (0,0) and input moves the player on the next physics frame. Repeat for falling and jumping deaths.
forms.explanation: Residual physics state causes inconsistent controls and can chain deaths, which undermines the respawn system.

criterion: Preserves the player jump counter value across death and respawn.

forms.assessment_process: Collect jump upgrades, note the current jump counter, then die and confirm the value is unchanged after respawn. Verify via UI or debug output if present.
forms.explanation: Persisting collected progression prevents frustration and matches the prompt requirement to keep what was collected before dying.

criterion: Reads the spawn point position from a scene node at runtime, so editor moves change respawn without code edits.

forms.assessment_process: Move the SpawnPoint instance in platformer.tscn, run the game, die, and confirm respawn occurs at the new location. Confirm no code changes were made.
forms.explanation: A single source of truth for spawn reduces maintenance and prevents mismatches between level layout and player start.

criterion: Sets player position to the spawn point and resets velocity to (0,0) on respawn.

forms.assessment_process: Trigger death and compare the player global position to the SpawnPoint global position. Inspect velocity values immediately after respawn to confirm both axes are zero.
forms.explanation: Exact repositioning and velocity reset prevent physics glitches and ensure consistent, fair respawns.

criterion: Routes fall, enemy contact, and projectile hit into one shared death handler in player.gd.

forms.assessment_process: Inspect player.gd for a single death function and confirm each death source triggers it. Verify no duplicated respawn code exists in enemy or projectile scripts.
forms.explanation: Centralizing death logic prevents drift between death types and reduces bugs when respawn behavior changes.

criterion: Triggers player death on shooter projectile hit and removes the prior placeholder print statement.

forms.assessment_process: Get hit by a shooter projectile and confirm immediate respawn at SpawnPoint. Search the shooter or projectile script for the old print and confirm it is gone.
forms.explanation: Projectile death is a core requirement and removing placeholder output confirms the feature is implemented, not stubbed.

criterion: Triggers player death on chaser body contact and removes the prior placeholder print statement.

forms.assessment_process: Touch the chaser enemy and confirm immediate respawn at SpawnPoint. Search the chaser script for the old print and confirm it is gone.
forms.explanation: Enemy contact death is a core requirement and placeholder removal ensures the interaction is fully wired.

criterion: Exposes a tunable fall death threshold in the editor and triggers respawn when the player crosses it.

forms.assessment_process: Adjust the threshold in the Inspector, run, and confirm falling below it triggers respawn. Confirm no code edits are needed to change the value.
forms.explanation: Editor tunability supports level iteration and prevents hardcoded values from breaking when layouts change.

criterion: Includes spawn_point.tscn and instances it in platformer.tscn, with the player starting at the same position.

forms.assessment_process: Confirm spawn_point.tscn exists and is instanced in platformer.tscn. Run and verify the player initial global position matches the SpawnPoint global position.
forms.explanation: A dedicated spawn scene and single source of truth prevents mismatched start positions and simplifies future checkpoint expansion.

criterion: Runs in Godot 4.x with zero errors or warnings in the debugger console during gameplay.

forms.assessment_process: Open the project in Godot 4.x, run the main scene, and trigger each death type. Confirm the debugger shows no errors or warnings.
forms.explanation: A clean console is a baseline quality bar and prevents hidden runtime issues from shipping into later tasks.
